svg.text: Naked single: a cell has only one candidate digit → place it.
Hidden single: within a row/col/box, a digit appears as candidate in only one cell → place it.
Naked pair: two cells in a unit share the same 2 candidates → eliminate those digits from the unit's other cells.
Hidden pair / triple: 2 (or 3) digits restricted to 2 (or 3) cells in a unit.
Pointing pair: a digit's candidates within a box are confined to one row/column → eliminate from rest of that line.
X-Wing / Swordfish: rectangular candidate patterns rule out digits in crossing rows and columns.
• MRV: branch on the cell with fewest candidates. Failing earlier = smaller search tree.
• LCV: within a cell, try the value that least constrains other cells.
• AC-3 / Forward Checking: after placing a digit, propagate eliminations through neighbours.
• Dancing Links (Knuth): represent Sudoku as exact-cover; DLX backtracking with O(1) cover/uncover.
• Norvig's solver (2006): combines constraint propagation + DFS, ~50 lines of Python, solves all 50
Project Euler #96 puzzles in <1s and most "world's hardest" Sudokus.
Pure DFS without propagation: 9^81 ≈ 10^77 states. With propagation: nearly always <10⁴ nodes visited.
